inputs.name: window.FileMaker
inputs.description: FileMaker WebViewer global object used to detect FileMaker environment

inputs.name: window.FMGofer
inputs.description: FileMaker bridge library for script execution

inputs.name: fetchDataFromFileMaker parameters
inputs.description: Layout, action, findCriteria, and other FileMaker operation parameters

inputs.name: axios request config
inputs.description: Request configuration with useFileMaker flag for routing decisions

outputs.name: environment detection result
outputs.description: Contains environmentType and authMethod after detection
outputs.assertion: should return WEBAPP environment type and SUPABASE auth method when window.FileMaker is undefined

outputs.name: backend API response
outputs.description: Response from backend API with data and metadata
outputs.assertion: should return data structure compatible with FileMaker response format when routing through backend

outputs.name: authentication state
outputs.assertion: should contain valid Supabase session with user ID and access token when using SUPABASE auth method

outputs.name: HMAC authorization header
outputs.description: Bearer token with HMAC-SHA256 signature and timestamp
outputs.assertion: should generate valid HMAC header in format 'Bearer {signature}.{timestamp}' for backend requests

outputs.name: data service routing decision
outputs.description: Routing path chosen by axios interceptor
outputs.assertion: should route to backend API when environmentType is WEBAPP and useFileMaker flag is false

outputs.name: FileMaker integration removal status
outputs.description: Verification that FileMaker code has been removed
outputs.assertion: should return no matches when grepping codebase for FileMaker references after removal

scenarios.name: Environment detection without FileMaker
scenarios.description: Verify app correctly detects web app environment when FileMaker objects are absent
steps: Load SignIn component without window.FileMaker object
Trigger environment detection in detectFileMakerEnvironment()
Check AppStateContext for environmentType and authMethod
scenarios.expectedOutcome: App sets environmentType to WEBAPP and authMethod to SUPABASE without attempting FileMaker authentication

scenarios.name: Backend API routing for customer operations
scenarios.description: Verify customer CRUD operations route through backend API instead of FileMaker
steps: Call fetchCustomers() from customers.js API module
Verify axios interceptor routes request to backend
Check that HMAC authorization header is present
Verify response matches expected customer data structure
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Request goes to /filemaker/devCustomers/records with HMAC auth and returns customer array

scenarios.name: Supabase-only authentication flow
scenarios.description: Verify authentication uses only Supabase without FileMaker auto-detection
steps: Load SignIn component
Verify no 3-second FileMaker detection delay
Submit email/password credentials
Check Supabase session creation
scenarios.expectedOutcome: User authenticates via Supabase immediately without FileMaker detection wait

scenarios.name: Remove FileMaker bridge files
scenarios.description: Verify all FileMaker bridge files are removed from codebase
steps: Check that src/api/fileMaker.js is deleted
Verify useFileMakerBridge hook is removed from src/hooks/index.js
Confirm FMGofer references are removed from dataService.js
Run grep for 'fetchDataFromFileMaker' and verify no matches
scenarios.expectedOutcome: FileMaker bridge implementation files and hooks are completely removed

scenarios.name: Remove environment detection logic
scenarios.description: Verify environment detection and routing logic is simplified to single path
steps: Check that ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER constant is removed
Verify axios interceptors no longer check useFileMaker flag
Confirm dataService.js uses only backend API routing
Verify initializationService.js removes FileMaker detection
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All dual-environment routing logic is removed and app assumes web app context

scenarios.name: Backend API compatibility
scenarios.description: Verify backend API supports all operations previously done via FileMaker
steps: Test CRUD operations for customers, projects, tasks, teams
Verify financial records sync operations work
Test notes and links operations
Confirm all operations return expected data structures
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All 60+ operations documented in BACKEND_API_REQUIREMENTS.md work correctly

scenarios.name: Project builds without FileMaker dependencies
scenarios.description: Verify project builds successfully after removing FileMaker code
steps: Run 'npm run build' command
Check for FileMaker-related build errors
Verify no missing FileMaker module imports
Confirm build output is valid
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Build completes successfully with no FileMaker-related errors or warnings

scenarios.name: Acceptance criteria verification script
scenarios.description: Run automated verification script to check all acceptance criteria
steps: Execute verify-filemaker-removal.sh script
Review output for each of 40 acceptance criteria
Check that score meets 38/40 minimum threshold
Verify zero failures on critical criteria
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Script passes with 38+ criteria met and all critical requirements satisfied

scenarios.name: HMAC authentication for all backend requests
scenarios.description: Verify all backend API requests include valid HMAC authorization
steps: Intercept axios requests to backend API
Verify Authorization header format matches 'Bearer {signature}.{timestamp}'
Confirm signature is valid HMAC-SHA256
Test that requests without HMAC are rejected
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All backend requests include valid HMAC auth and unauthorized requests fail

scenarios.name: Remove FileMaker config and constants
scenarios.description: Verify FileMaker configuration and constants are removed
steps: Check src/config.js for FileMaker URL/database config removal
Verify VITE_FM_* environment variables are not referenced
Confirm FM_LAYOUTS constants are removed
Check that FM_ACTIONS constants are removed
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All FileMaker configuration, environment variables, and constants are removed

scenarios.name: UI components work without FileMaker context
scenarios.description: Verify UI components render and function correctly without FileMaker environment
steps: Load CustomerDetails component
Load ProjectDetails component
Test ProposalCreationForm
Verify all components fetch data via backend API
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All UI components function correctly using only backend API data sources

scenarios.name: Rollback procedure validation
scenarios.description: Verify rollback procedure can restore FileMaker functionality if needed
steps: Document current git commit SHA
Test git revert to previous FileMaker-enabled commit
Verify npm install restores dependencies
Confirm rollback completes within 5-minute window
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Rollback procedure successfully restores previous FileMaker-enabled version

scenarios.name: Zero FileMaker references in codebase
scenarios.description: Verify comprehensive removal of all FileMaker references from source code
steps: Run grep -r 'FileMaker' src/ and verify zero matches
Run grep -r 'FMGofer' src/ and verify zero matches
Run grep -r 'fetchDataFromFileMaker' src/ and verify zero matches
Run grep -r 'ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER' src/ and verify zero matches
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Codebase contains zero references to FileMaker objects, functions, or constants

scenarios.name: Backend change requests implemented
scenarios.description: Verify all backend change requests (BCR-001 through BCR-005) are implemented
steps: Test BCR-001: Convert /filemaker/* endpoints to query Supabase
Test BCR-002: Products and subscriptions endpoints
Test BCR-003: Financial records with QuickBooks sync
Test BCR-004: Notes, links, relationships
Test BCR-005: dapiRecords table operations
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All backend change requests are implemented and tested

scenarios.name: Monitoring and alerting active
scenarios.description: Verify monitoring captures metrics and alerts fire correctly post-deployment
steps: Check that error rate monitoring is active
Verify authentication failure alerts work
Confirm API latency monitoring is capturing metrics
Test that alert thresholds trigger notifications
scenarios.expectedOutcome: All monitoring metrics are captured and alerts fire within defined thresholds
